description: Row-Level Security role definitions for Clearwater Health Power BI semantic model. Replaces legacy per-report SSRS folder-based permissions and Crystal Reports client-side security with centralized dataset-level RLS.

roles.name: DepartmentRole
roles.description: Department managers and staff see only financial and operational data for their assigned department. Replaces SSRS folder permissions on RevenueCycle reports where each department folder was individually permissioned.

tablePermissions.explanation: Filters the Departments dimension table so users only see rows matching their Azure AD UPN. Because Departments has a one-to-many relationship to Charges (via department_id), this filter propagates through the star schema to restrict Charges, Payments, and Adjustments automatically.

legacyEquivalent.mechanism: Folder-based permissions on /RevenueCycle/ folder tree. Each department had a subfolder with NTLM group-based access.
legacyEquivalent.storedProcedure: usp_GetDailyRevenue (no user filtering — returned all departments)

roles.name: PhysicianRole
roles.description: Attending physicians see only clinical data for their own patients. Replaces Crystal Reports client-side filtering where the Oracle REPORTS_USER account returned all patients and client-side record selection formulas restricted visibility.

tablePermissions.explanation: Filters the Patients table so physicians only see patients where attending_physician matches their Azure AD UPN. This filter propagates through relationships to Surgeries, Complications, and ward-level census data. Uses LOOKUPVALUE to validate the user email against the attending_physician column.

legacyEquivalent.mechanism: PatientCensus.rpt used Oracle REPORTS_USER to pull all patients; client-side record selection formula filtered by physician. No server-side enforcement existed — all patient data was transmitted to the client.
legacyEquivalent.securityGap: Crystal Reports transmitted all patient rows to the client before filtering. Power BI RLS enforces filtering server-side, improving HIPAA compliance.

roles.name: ExecutiveRole
roles.description: Executive leadership (CFO, CEO, Quality Director, Compliance Officers) have unrestricted access to all data across all departments, patients, and quality metrics. Replaces the legacy pattern where executives had folder-level access to all SSRS report folders and direct Crystal Reports viewer access.

legacyEquivalent.mechanism: SSRS: Domain Admins group granted access to all report folders. Crystal: Direct access to Crystal Reports viewer application with no record selection restrictions.
legacyEquivalent.note: No filter expression needed — empty tablePermissions array means no RLS restrictions apply.

securityUserMappingTable.description: Optional lookup table for environments where USERPRINCIPALNAME() does not directly match the department_name or attending_physician column values. Create a SecurityUserMapping table in the semantic model to map UPNs to security attributes.

columns.name: role_type
columns.description: Role assignment: Department, Physician, or Executive

deploymentNotes.powerBIDesktop: Define roles in Modeling tab → Manage Roles. Paste DAX filter expressions from rls-dax-filters.dax.
deploymentNotes.powerBIService: After publishing, assign Azure AD users/groups to roles in Dataset Settings → Row-Level Security.
deploymentNotes.testingApproach: Use 'View as Role' in Power BI Desktop and 'Test as Role' in Power BI Service. See test-matrix.md for detailed test cases.
deploymentNotes.azureADRequirement: RLS with USERPRINCIPALNAME() requires Azure AD authentication. SQL authentication will return BLANK() for USERPRINCIPALNAME().
